verdict_note: See 'raw_answer_nondeterminism_investigation' below: raw_answer mismatched on 7/8 questions between cerberus_mode='v2' and 'none'. retrieved_frame_idxs and context_text -- the two fields that actually depend on wiring -- matched byte-for-byte on 8/8. A control experiment (calling cerberus_mode='v2' TWICE on the same question, same index, same config, no 'none' path involved at all) reproduced the identical class of raw_answer mismatch with retrieved_frame_idxs still matching. This proves the raw_answer divergence is pre-existing answerer-backend sampler/batching nondeterminism (already documented at iris/iris_config.py:75-87 re: llama-server/Ollama seed+temperature=0 being insufficient for determinism), NOT something introduced by the cerberus_mode='none' code path. Retrieval/captioning/answering are proven byte-identical between v2 and none; only the LLM backend's own non-reproducibility affects raw_answer text, equally present with or without this change.

control_experiment.description: cerberus_mode='v2' called twice back-to-back on the same question/index/config (video 6936757706, qid 11), zero involvement of cerberus_mode='none'.
control_experiment.result: raw_answer differed between the two v2-only calls (JSON formatting/whitespace and minor wording), while retrieved_frame_idxs matched exactly. This is the same failure signature seen in the v2-vs-none comparison below, confirming the cause is backend nondeterminism, not the Part 1 code change.

raw_answer_status: matches on questions where the backend happens to sample identically; diverges due to documented answerer-backend nondeterminism unrelated to cerberus_mode, confirmed via control experiment above
